commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
This PR removes the Workboard mini-game and rewires Workboard starts, stops, lifecycle display, docs, i18n, and tests around Gateway task-backed board runs.

PR surface: Source -387, Tests +469, Docs +19. Total +101 across 50 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: the PR builds a bare `subagent:` key for unassigned cards, and the current Gateway resolves that kind of key to `main` before consulting configured defaults.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow controller/test repair is needed before merge because the current diff can misroute unassigned Workboard starts.

target.title: fix(workboard): wire task-backed board runs
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Workboard still had Peter-era prototype debris: a mini-game surface, unclear dispatch/nudge copy, and autonomous starts that were not reliably anchored to the Gateway task ledger.
- This PR removes the game, makes autonomous Workboard card starts task-backed through `agent`, and keeps card/task/session lifecycle state from drifting after starts, stops, manual opens, and stale task refreshes.

Why does this matter now?

- Users need the board to represent real work. A Kanban-style surface that can show fake or stale run state is worse than no board because it teaches operators not to trust it.

What is the intended outcome?

- Autonomous Workboard runs create Gateway tasks, stop through task cancellation, preserve the configured default agent unless a card is explicitly assigned, and hide admin-only model overrides from non-admin operators.
- Dispatch UI now shows a concrete dispatch summary instead of a vague nudge affordance.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- This does not make Workboard a full task-management product yet. The next layer should add richer queue semantics, explicit dispatch previews/action history, better detail views, and board hygiene controls.

What does success look like?

- A card started from Workboard can be traced through task/run/session state, stopped from the board, and rendered from authoritative task status when task data exists.

What should reviewers focus on?

- `ui/src/ui/controllers/workboard.ts` task/session lifecycle transitions.
- `extensions/workboard/src/dispatcher.ts` session-key behavior for assigned vs unassigned cards.
- Whether the UI copy now makes dispatch behavior legible without overclaiming.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- Maintainer-requested cleanup of the Workboard/Kanban surface.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Workboard prototype debris, fake game UI, unclear dispatch copy, and card starts that were not consistently wired to Gateway tasks.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw gwt worktree with delegated Blacksmith Testbox changed gate.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: See Tests and validation.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Focused Workboard Vitest suites passed, Control UI production build passed, and delegated Testbox `check:changed` passed in run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26727247562.
- Observed result after fix: Workboard card starts now use task-backed autonomous Gateway runs, task-linked stops cancel tasks, stale task links are cleared, and the mini-game surface is gone.
- What was not tested: A live browser click-through against a real Gateway task queue in this PR pass.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: `scripts/control-ui-i18n.ts check` is currently blocked on latest-main `ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts` raw-copy baseline drift unrelated to this branch. The branch avoids committing that unrelated raw-copy baseline churn.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Existing Workboard UI included the leftover mini-game and dispatch/nudge affordance with no task-backed lifecycle contract.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --no-web-search` - clean, no accepted/actionable findings.
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --label workboard-check-changed -- env OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 corepack pnpm check:changed` - passed on Testbox `tbx_01kt04ybwvvz61w2erpm8kvv53`, GitHub Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26727247562, before the final locale-refresh rebase.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.ui.config.ts --configLoader runner ui/src/ui/controllers/workboard.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/workboard.test.ts ui/src/styles/workboard.test.ts` - passed after the final rebase, 67 tests.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/workboard/src/dispatcher.test.ts extensions/workboard/src/command.test.ts extensions/workboard/src/gateway.test.ts` - passed after the final rebase, 9 tests.
- `OPENCLAW_BUILD_ALL_NO_PNPM=1 node scripts/ui.js build` - passed after the final rebase.
- `node scripts/docs-list.js` - passed.
- `node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check` - passed.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` - passed.

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Workboard controller/view/style tests cover task-backed card starts, task cancellation, stale task cleanup, dispatch summary copy, admin-only model overrides, and the removed game surface.
- Workboard dispatcher tests cover default-agent-safe unassigned subagent keys and assigned-card scoped keys.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The cleanup target was product drift rather than a single failing test: the board exposed non-work game UI and could show/run cards without a durable task-backed contract.
- After the final rebase, `PATH="/tmp/openclaw-pnpmshim:$PATH" node --import tsx scripts/control-ui-i18n.ts check` fails on unrelated latest-main `ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts` raw-copy baseline drift.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added/updated for the changed Workboard controller, view, style, and dispatcher behavior.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Card lifecycle reconciliation between Gateway sessions and task ledger state.

How is that risk mitigated?

- Keep manual starts on the existing session path, use Gateway `agent` only for autonomous card starts, prefer task cancellation when task state exists, and test stale run/task/session edge cases directly.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Review task/session lifecycle semantics and run CI.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- CI and an optional live Gateway/browser click-through if reviewers want real dashboard proof before merge.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- Local autoreview findings were addressed until the patch came back clean.
target.author: vincentkoc

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
